widgets_values:
Photorealistic cinematic portrait of Din Djarin (The Mandalorian) and Grogu from Star Wars, captured in ultra-high detail as if shot on a high-end movie camera with natural dramatic lighting. Din Djarin stands in full beskar armor: gleaming silver helmet with T-visor reflecting subtle environmental glow, dark underlayer fabric visible at joints, brown leather cape draped over one shoulder, Amban phase-pulse rifle slung across his back, bandolier with ammo pouches across chest, heavy blaster pistol holstered at hip, rugged leather gloves, boots planted firmly in a heroic stance. His pose is protective and stoic, one armored hand gently supporting Grogu who sits in the crook of his other arm.
Grogu (Baby Yoda), small green alien infant with large expressive dark eyes, wrinkled skin textured with fine details and subtle moisture, tiny pointed ears, soft fuzzy hair tufts on head, wearing his signature beige robe with coarse fabric folds, is holding a small white rectangular sign in both tiny clawed hands raised proudly toward the camera. The sign is hand-lettered in bold black capital letters: "BASE" (simple, slightly uneven marker writing for a cute, child-made feel). Grogu's expression is wide-eyed innocent curiosity mixed with pride, mouth slightly open in a soft coo, big eyes looking directly at viewer.
Setting: dimly lit Mandalorian ship interior or neutral sci-fi environment with soft blue ambient lighting from control panels, warm highlights from overhead spots catching on beskar and Grogu's skin, subtle dust motes in air, shallow depth of field with creamy bokeh background, cinematic color grading—cool metallic tones balanced by warm skin highlights on Grogu. Hyper-realistic textures: scratched and weathered beskar with realistic reflections and micro-scratches, lifelike skin pores and wrinkles on Grogu, individual fabric threads on robe and cape, metallic sheen on armor, natural subsurface scattering on green skin.
Amateur-to-professional photo style: slight lens flare from lights, natural grain, high dynamic range with deep shadows and bright specular highlights on helmet, photorealistic 8K ultra-detailed rendering, aspect ratio 16:9 cinematic or 3:4 vertical portrait, --v 6 --q 2 --stylize 400 --chaos 10 --style raw

The second step requires a two part skin regeneration process which is driven by the users settings. The outputs above are the results of the higher detail pass using Z-image-turbo.

This is the first step in the refinement process - a soft upscale to create a new foundation for the generation. Helps remove artifacts and create a solid core for realism. Main purpose is to standardizze the type of input images provided to the workflow, allowing for (in theory) a broad range of input images.

nodes.title: Detail Level Explanation
widgets_values: Controls the overall detail level of your image. Values should remain between .01 and .15 for best performance. Values over .12 may yield unrealistic results. However, when paired with smoothness below, high detail values can lead to really unique, interesting skin combinations.
Optional SeedVR2 upscaling for high resolution!

nodes.title: Smoothness/Softness Explanation
widgets_values: The smoothness/softness setting is the key driver of image blending between heavy and light skin regenerations. Lower values will typically yield softer, cleaner results when paired with high detail values.

nodes.title: Face Mask Blur Explanation
widgets_values: The face mask blur section can be optionally adjusted based on your final results. Sometimes, masking can slip by a few pixels depending on your input image. In this event, you can make the mask tighter or wider by adapting the Face Mask Blur settings. Generally, leave this at default for initial generations and test from there.

nodes.title: A: Pre-Hair Fix   B: Post-Hair Fix
💇 Money-Piece Hair Detailer — Reference
widgets_values:
# 💇 Hair Detailer - Money-Piece Lock

## Was macht dieser Block?

Dieser FaceDetailer ist auf **Haare** umfunktioniert. Er nutzt ein Hair-YOLO-
Segmentation-Model statt `face_yolov8m`, targettet die Haar-Region und re-sampled
sie mit dem `sinaneu` Character LoRA + einem Money-Piece-spezifischen Prompt.
Ziel: die bilateral symmetrische platinblonde Front-Strähne wiederherstellen
wenn Z-Image sie asymmetrisch rendert (ein Seite blond, andere dunkel).

## Wie das funktioniert

Der Character LoRA `sinaneu_000002700` wurde auf Sina mit korrektem Money-Piece
trainiert. Wenn die Haar-Region mit diesem LoRA re-gesampled wird, reasserted
er das gelernte Muster — gleiche Technik wie der Character Face Detailer,
nur auf Haare angewendet.

## Erforderliche Model-Files

- **`segm/hair_yolov8n-seg_60.pt`** - Hair Segmentation (empfohlen)
  - Download: huggingface.co/Bingsu/adetailer/blob/main/hair_yolov8n-seg_60.pt
  - Nach: `ComfyUI/models/ultralytics/segm/`
  
- **Fallbacks** falls das nicht funktioniert:
  - `person_yolov8m-seg.pt` (segmentiert ganze Person inkl. Haare)
  - `bbox/face_yolov8m.pt` mit grossem `bbox_dilation` von 50+ (cheesy aber tut)

## Tuning-Knobs (am Hair Detailer Node)

- **denoise: 0.40** (aktuell)
  - Runter auf 0.30: minimalerer Eingriff, weniger Identitätsverlust, aber
    asymmetrisches Muster kann bleiben
  - Hoch auf 0.55-0.60: garantiert symmetrisches Muster, aber Haare werden
    stärker umgeschrieben, könnten vom Input abweichen
    
- **bbox_dilation: 12** (aktuell)
  - Erweitert die Hair Bbox etwas um sicher alle Haarsträhnen zu fassen
  - Rauf wenn Haare am Rand des Crops abgeschnitten werden
  
- **sam_dilation: 4** (aktuell)
  - Expandiert die SAM Mask um den Übergang Haut-zu-Haar sauberer zu machen
  - Rauf (6-8) bei weichem Mask-Übergang, runter (0-2) bei scharfem

## Bypass

Nicht jedes Bild braucht den Hair Fix. Rechtsklick auf den Hair Detailer Node 
→ Bypass wenn das Money-Piece schon korrekt generiert wurde. Der Final Save 
geht dann durch und zeigt das Character-Locked Output unverändert.

## Output-Saves

1. `Silent Snow RSV5 Character-Locked Final` (intermediate, post Face Detailer)
2. `Silent Snow RSV5 FINAL Character+MoneyPiece Locked` (final, post Hair Detailer)

Und ein Image Comparer direkt darunter zeigt A/B.
